title: You are main-orchestrator. Issue Title: Happy auth-stale recovery final completion review Issue Body: Review the already...
summary_text: {"risk":"MEDIUM","approve":false,"findings":["apps/happy-web/src/app.js:114-125 and :166-174 persist resume state without guarding localStorage.setItem; if storage writes throw, the auth-stale primary CTA can fail before redirect on Safari/PWA-class clients.","apps/happy-web/auth/auth.js:121-130 plus apps/happy-web/src/domain/happy-auth-session.js:10-22 assume token persistence cannot fail; a successful password login can be reported as failure and skip the Happy redirect if storage writes throw. apps/happy-web/src/adapters/happy-endpoint-client.js:112-115 makes the same assumption for shared refresh locking.","apps/happy-web/src/domain/happy-auth-gateway.js:5-12 treats any /happy/* path as safe returnTo, including /happy/auth/*, so the 'safe returnTo restore' still allows auth-page self-loops after login.","The local-direct Claude restore path in apps/happy-web/src/app.js:1004-1011 is not flow-covered end to end; current coverage proves URL composition, but not a live-route round trip back into restored Happy state."],"recommendation":"Do not mark this fully complete yet. Harden storage-write failure handling around resume/token persistence and shared auth locks, exclude /happy/auth from allowed returnTo targets, and add one integration test for the live-route returnTo round trip. After that, the remaining work can reasonably be limited to manual Safari/PWA smoke checks.","rationale":"The implemented scope is close and the Happy-web auth/PWA checks I reran passed locally: test-auth-reentry, test-auth-gateway, test-auth-gateway-runtime, test-auth-stale-reauth-smoke, test-endpoint-client, test-behavior-contract, test-state-contract, test-view-contract, and test-pwa-shell. But the remaining gaps are code-level, not just manual-smoke residuals, and they directly affect the claimed auth-stale recovery and safe returnTo completion."}
